name: George Gelnovatch
record: 550+ W, 9 College Cups, 7 NCAA Championships
bio: George Gelnovatch is the most decorated active head coach in US college soccer. He has been at Virginia since 1995 — 30 years — building the winningest program in NCAA history. His coaching tree includes Aidan Morris (Columbus Crew, USMNT), Claudio Reyna (USMNT captain), John Harkes, and Tony Meola — players who define the history of American soccer. Gelnovatch is known for a high-pressing, technically sophisticated system that mirrors European club football. He prepares players for professional careers more than any other active college coach. His playing style perfectly suits a composed, box-to-box 8 with range and pressing intensity.

staff.name: Matt Chulis
staff.bg: 20th season on UVA staff 2025, 14th as Assoc HC. 3x UVA All-American. Former MLS: Columbus Crew, Chicago Fire, Portland Timbers. Instrumental in both 2009 and 2014 NCAA Championship runs.

staff.name: Johnny Fenwick
staff.bg: Hired April 2026. Joins from High Point University (Director of Player Development 2025). Previously assistant and director of operations at High Point.

name: Mike Noonan
record: 2× NCAA National Champion (2021, 2023). 400+ career wins. 4 first-round MLS picks in 2026 draft alone. Nation-leading pipeline.
bio: Mike Noonan is in his 16th season at Clemson and is widely regarded as one of the elite coaches in college soccer. His 2021 and 2023 NCAA championships make him the only active coach with 2+ recent national titles. Noonan's high-press, technically demanding style has produced an extraordinary professional pipeline — 4 first-round picks in the 2026 draft alone including #4 overall Kwaku Agyabeng. Robbie Robinson won the Hermann Trophy and went #1 overall in 2020 under Noonan. Riggs Field is one of the most iconic venues in college soccer.

staff.name: Gaye Diadie
staff.bg: 1st season — fresh perspective on the staff.

name: Sasho Cirovski
record: 3× NCAA National Champion (2005, 2008, 2018). 49 MLS picks + 7 homegrown. 6 players at FIFA World Cup. 2018 National Coach of Year. Led nation in attendance 2016, 2019, 2025.
bio: Sasho Cirovski is the most decorated active coach in college soccer history by professional pipeline — 49 MLS SuperDraft picks, 7 MLS homegrown signings, and 6 players who appeared at FIFA Men's World Cups. His 2018 national championship was built on a possession-based, technically demanding system that produced Zack Steffen (USMNT World Cup goalkeeper), Graham Zusi (2014 World Cup goal), Omar Gonzalez, and Taylor Twellman. Ludwig Field leads the nation in attendance because Maryland soccer fans are among the most passionate in the country. In 32 seasons, Cirovski has built Maryland into college soccer's most prolific professional development machine.
strengths: 49 MLS picks — most of any coach

staff.name: Jake Pace
staff.bg: Maryland alumnus. B.S. Kinesiology from Maryland. Back-to-back ACC champion as player 2012-13.

name: Brian Wiese
record: 2019 NCAA National Champion. 42 MLS signings all-time. 6× East Regional Staff of Year. 14 NCAA Tournament berths in 16yr span.
bio: Brian Wiese is in his 20th season at Georgetown and has built the Hoyas into one of the most consistent programs in college soccer. The 2019 national championship capped a historic run — Georgetown's first ever title, won with a 20-1-3 record and a dramatic penalty shootout against Virginia. Wiese has sent 42 players to MLS and been named East Regional Staff of Year 6 times. His possession-based, technically sophisticated style suits a midfielder like Olivier. Georgetown's Washington D.C. location provides unmatched clinical placement access for DPT preparation.

staff.name: Connor Klekota
staff.bg: Notre Dame 2013 NCAA champion as player under Bobby Clark.

name: Todd Yeagley
record: 16th season 2026. 2012 NCAA National Champion. 11 Big Ten titles. 4 consecutive Big Ten Tourn 2020-23. 39 MLS draftees.
bio: Todd Yeagley enters his 16th season at Indiana in 2026. In 2012 he led the Hoosiers to the NCAA National Championship as a #16 seed — the first and only time a #16 seed has won the championship. His staff includes Christian Lomeli, Tanner Thompson, and Jacob Bushue as assistant coaches (all reachable at 812-855-0051). Son of legendary Jerry Yeagley who won 5 titles. Former Columbus Crew MLS player. 11 Hoosiers are in MLS as of the 2026 season.

name: Ryan Jorden
record: 7th season 2025. 150-113-35 (.562). 2025 Big Ten Tournament Champions. 13 MLS draftees.
bio: Ryan Jorden enters his 7th season at UCLA in 2025. He won the 2025 Big Ten Tournament, defeating Michigan 5-0 in the final. Holds a PhD in Biomechanics from Northern Colorado. His 2026 staff includes Sergi Nus and Kary Whitney as assistant coaches.

name: Chad Riley
record: 2023 NCAA Runner-Up. 2021 ACC Tournament Champions. Maintained Bobby Clark's elite standard. College Cup 2021, 2023.
bio: Chad Riley inherited one of the most prestigious programs in college soccer from legendary coach Bobby Clark and has maintained Notre Dame's elite status. Riley's 2023 team finished as NCAA runners-up (13-3-6, ACC Regular Season Champions) and his 2021 squad won the ACC Tournament and reached the College Cup. Notre Dame's program has produced 27 MLS draft picks under Clark and continues that pipeline under Riley. The Fighting Irish play in the ACC — the toughest conference in college soccer — making every season a gauntlet of top-25 opponents.

name: Kyle Russell
record: 7th season 2026. 2021 CUSA CoY. 3 AAC Championship Finals in 4 seasons (2022,2024,2025). 14 MLS draftees.
bio: Kyle Russell enters his 7th season at FIU in 2026. In 2025 he reached his second consecutive AAC Championship final — outlasting #1 Charlotte in a dramatic PK shootout in the semis. His staff from the official 2026 roster: Dannie Merida (Associate HC), Matteo Gasperoni (Assistant), Giuseppe Weller (Volunteer GK Coach).

staff.name: Dannie Merida
staff.bg: Associate Head Coach. Recruiting and player development.

name: Tim Vom Steeg
record: 27th season 2025. Career 324-152-76. 2006 NCAA champion. 32 MLS draftees including Manu Duah #1 overall 2025.
bio: Tim Vom Steeg enters his 27th season at UCSB in 2025 as the longest-tenured coach in this guide. His four sons all played for UCSB. His 2025 staff: Greg Wilson (Assoc HC), Matias Fernandez (GK Coach), Carson Vom Steeg (Asst — former US U-18 captain and Stanford NCAA champion), Kian Kohan (Director of Operations). Email: tvomsteeg@athletics.ucsb.edu

staff.name: Greg Wilson
staff.bg: Associate Head Coach. Recruiting and player development.

name: Kevin Hudson
record: 11th season 2026. 115-44-23 (.693). 2025 ACC Tournament Champions. NCAA Elite Eight 2024.
bio: Kevin Hudson enters his 11th season at SMU in 2026 as the 2025 ACC Tournament champion coach — SMU's first ever ACC title. His staff (as of Feb 2026): Michael King (Assoc HC, Recruiting), Jaime Ibarra-Perez (Assoc HC, GK — both promoted Feb 2026), Ross Fitzpatrick (Assistant), Alejandro Radilla (Director Sports Science).

staff.name: Michael King
staff.bg: Associate Head Coach. Recruiting and player development.

staff.name: Jaime Ibarra-Perez
staff.bg: Associate Head Coach. Recruiting and player development.

staff.name: Alejandro Radilla
staff.role: Director of Sports Science and Analytics

name: Carlos Somoano
record: 2023 ACC Regular Season Champions. 14 NCAA Tournament appearances. Consistent top-25. Dorrance dynasty legacy program.
bio: Carlos Somoano has maintained UNC's place among the elite of college soccer in his 14 seasons — a remarkable feat given the Anson Dorrance women's dynasty that dominates the university's soccer identity. Somoano's 2023 team won the ACC Regular Season title and he consistently produces ACC All-Conference players and MLS draftees. UNC's Exercise and Sport Science degree is explicitly designed for DPT, OT and PA school preparation — making it one of the strongest academic fits in the guide for Olivier. Chapel Hill's Research Triangle location provides world-class clinical access through UNC Health and Duke Medical.
strengths: UNC Health + Duke Medical clinical access

staff.name: Grant Porter
staff.bg: 14-year tenure alongside Somoano — longest-serving assistant in ACC.

name: Jared Embick
record: Contract extended through 2035. Multiple MAC titles, 2021 College Cup (Final Four), consistent NCAA Tournament program.
bio: Jared Embick has built Akron into one of the most consistent D1 programs in America. Contract extended through 2035 — total institutional commitment. Under his staff (Associate HC Ger Coppinger, 11th season), Akron has produced 18 MLS draft picks including Emil Jaaskelainen (#7 overall Inter Miami 2024), won multiple MAC titles, and reached the 2021 College Cup Final Four. USSF A License. High-intensity pressing style with strong technical development focus. The Pre-PT pipeline through Cleveland Clinic is a genuine USP Embick has built around the program.

staff.name: Ger Coppinger
staff.bg: 11th season at Akron. 18 MLS picks in his tenure.

name: George Kiefer
record: Begins 2nd USF tenure 2026. First tenure 2002-16: 162-86-51. Career 230-151-82 across 24 seasons.
bio: George Kiefer begins his second tenure at USF in 2026, announced Dec 10 2025. He brings his GCU assistants with him — Daniel De Oliveira and AJ Madero (both helped win 2025 WAC title and reach NCAA Sweet 16 at GCU). Armanté Marshall (former USF player under Kiefer) also stays on as Associate HC.

staff.name: Daniel De Oliveira
staff.bg: Associate Head Coach. Recruiting and player development.

staff.name: Armanté Marshall
staff.bg: Associate Head Coach. Recruiting and player development.

name: Jim Barlow
record: 29th season 2025. 237-184-72 career. 7 Ivy titles. First-ever Ivy double (title + tournament) 2025. Northeast Regional Staff of Year 2025.
bio: Jim Barlow enters his 29th season at Princeton in 2025 fresh from his greatest season — the first-ever Ivy League + Tournament double, going 7-0 in conference without conceding a single goal. His staff: Steve Totten (Assoc HC, stotten@princeton.edu, 18 years on staff), Sam Maira (Asst/GK, smaira@princeton.edu, 5 years), Tom Moffat (Asst, 19 years). The full staff was named 2025 Northeast Regional Coaching Staff of Year.

staff.name: Steve Totten
staff.bg: Associate Head Coach. Recruiting and player development.

name: Bobby Muuss
record: 11th season 2025. 152-40-24 (first 10 seasons). 3 ACC titles (2016,2017,2024). 14 consecutive NCAA Tournaments.
bio: Bobby Muuss enters his 11th season at Wake Forest in 2025. His record of 152 wins in first 10 seasons is more than any D1 program in that span. 2026 staff: Dane Brenner (Assoc HC / Recruiting, brennerd@wfu.edu), Zack Schilawski (Asst / Player Development, Schilazj@wfu.edu), Ade Taiwo (Asst, taiwoa@wfu.edu). HC email: muussrp@wfu.edu

staff.name: Dane Brenner
staff.bg: Associate Head Coach / Director of Recruiting & Player Identification / Director of Goalkeeping.

staff.name: Zack Schilawski
staff.bg: Assistant Coach / Director of Player Development.

name: Lucas Martorana
record: HC record 33-22-9 (.586) in 4 seasons (interim 2022, HC from Nov 2022). 2025 SSC Coaching Staff of Year.
bio: Lucas Martorana is head coach at PBA since November 2022. His 2025 staff from the official roster: Pete Arnold, Jake Strachan, Leo Jean-Jacques, Guillermo Falo (all Assistant Coaches). Chuck Maddox Sr. also contributed to the 2025 SSC Staff of Year. Note: the 61-9-6 record often cited was as an ASSISTANT under the previous head coach McMahon, not as HC.

name: Brian Young
record: 168-104-32 (.605) in first 16-season tenure. 9 conference championships. 2024 LSC Coach of Year.
bio: Brian Young is the long-tenured head coach at St. Edwards — 16 seasons in his first stint before the program was shut down in 2020 (COVID), then reinstated in 2023 for his second tenure. 2024 LSC Coach of Year. Associate HC Corey Miller (promoted March 2025) has been on staff since 2017. Young was previously assistant at Brown University where he helped them to 4 Ivy League titles. Email: byoung@stedwards.edu

staff.name: Corey Miller
staff.bg: Promoted March 2025. On staff since 2017. USSF National B License. Former Simpson College captain.

name: Rob Dow
record: Named Penn State HC Dec 2025. At Vermont (2016-25): 129-41-21, 2024 NCAA National Champion, 5 straight NCAA Tournaments, 9th-best active win % (5+ seasons).
bio: Rob Dow was named Penn State's head coach in December 2025 after nine seasons at Vermont, where he compiled a 129-41-21 record, reached five consecutive NCAA Tournaments and won the 2024 NCAA National Championship. He holds the nation's ninth-best win percentage among active coaches with 5+ seasons and was back-to-back America East Coaching Staff of the Year (2024, 2025). Dow inherits Penn State's 2013 NCAA finalist program and a newly renovated $21.25M Jeffrey Field complex.

name: John Kerr
record: 30+ seasons at Duke. Multiple ACC titles. 300+ career wins.
bio: John Kerr Jr. is one of the longest-serving and most decorated coaches in college soccer history, entering his 31st season at Duke in 2025. His Duke program has produced consistent ACC contenders and professional players across three decades, including the 1986 NCAA Championship infrastructure he inherited and built upon. Kerr's teams are known for technical discipline and system-first soccer — a possession-based, positionally organised style that prioritises movement and combination play. Former Duke player himself, he returned as head coach in 1994 and has never left.

name: John Rootes
record: 194-54-31 (.751) at Lynn. Career 498-150-55 across 4 schools. 3 NCAA D2 National Championships (2012, 2014, 2024).
bio: John Rootes has been head coach at Lynn since 2008. His 2025 staff from the official roster: Anthony Desperito (Assistant Coach, email via program). Desperito is a former Lynn player who scored the game-winning goal in the 2012 national championship final and has now won 2 titles as a coach under Rootes. Career coaching record includes a 169-30-11 mark at SNHU and 1989 national championship. Email: jrootes@lynn.edu

staff.name: Anthony Desperito
staff.bg: Former Lynn player. Scored winning goal in 2012 championship as player. Won 2012, 2014, 2024 titles — 2 as player, 2 as coach.

name: Jamie Franks
record: 5 consecutive Summit League titles (2021-2025), 15 straight NCAA Tournaments, 2024 College Cup (Final Four).
bio: Jamie Franks is in his 10th season and has built Denver into the dominant Summit League program. Five consecutive conference titles, 15 straight NCAA Tournaments, and a College Cup Final Four appearance in 2024 — the best run in program history. Moving the program to the West Coast Conference in 2026 is a major step up in competition and national visibility. Known for developing technical midfielders (Cole Bassett to USMNT). One of the most underrated coaches in D1 soccer.

name: Petsa Ivanovic
record: 31-18-8 in 3 seasons. 2023 SSC regular season + tournament double — first time in program history.
bio: Petsa Ivanovic has been HC at Barry since spring 2022. Previously top assistant at Marshall for 5 years where he helped win the 2020-21 D1 NCAA National Championship. 2025 staff from official roster: Ivan Barriga (Assoc HC, ibarriga@barry.edu, 305-899-3135), Juan Wulf (GK Coach), Ethan Sonis and Jose Santamarina (Volunteer Assistants). Originally from Serbia, played D2 and D1 in USA.

staff.name: Ivan Barriga
staff.bg: Associate Head Coach. Recruiting and player development.

name: Brian Maisonneuve
record: 3 consecutive Big Ten titles 2022-24
bio: Brian Maisonneuve is a former MLS midfielder and US National Team player who has built Ohio State into a Big Ten power. Three consecutive Big Ten titles (2022, 2023, 2024) are the benchmark of his tenure. His professional playing background infuses the Ohio State program with elite tactical standards. Known for a high-energy pressing system that produces MLS prospects.

name: Billy Martin
record: HC since 2020-21. 2024: 14-2-4, SAC Tournament champs, NAIA Tournament 2nd round.
bio: Billy Martin returned to OCU as head coach in 2020-21. OCU alumnus — played for founder Brian Harvey 1989-91. Was Harvey assistant for 19 years before establishing Rose State mens soccer (74-35-2, 4 Region II titles). 2025 staff from official roster: Harvey Paul (Asst, hlpaul1@okcu.edu, former OCU player from Bristol England), Aiden Twohig (Asst), Thaddeus Caraway (GK Coach). Program URL updated to ocusports.com.

name: Jeremy Gunn
record: Former US U-20 National Team HC. Stanford ACC top-2 finish 2025.
bio: Jeremy Gunn joined Stanford as head coach after serving as the US U-20 National Team Head Coach and as part of the Colorado Rapids staff. His national team background and development-first philosophy fit Stanford's elite player-production model. At Stanford since ~2019, he has maintained the program's elite standing through the Pac-12 to ACC transition — reaching the 2025 ACC Tournament semi-final. Stanford's soccer environment produces professional players at the highest rate of any academic institution in the country.

name: Dr Dave Masur
record: 468-213-118 career (.660). 415-192-112 at SJU. 1996 NCAA champion. 2nd-winningest active D1 HC.
bio: Dr Dave Masur enters his 35th season at St Johns in 2025 — his 39th as a college HC overall. In 2025 the Red Storm returned to the NCAA Tournament for the first time since 2021 with a 9-6-4 record. Guilherme Gomes won Big East Freshman of Year with 9 goals. Associate HC David Janezic has been on staff since 2017 and has been named a Top D1 Assistant multiple times. Masur also holds the title of Associate Athletics Director.

staff.name: David Janezic
staff.bg: Associate Head Coach. Recruiting and player development.

name: Ian McIntyre
record: 2022 NCAA Champions. Multiple ACC tournament appearances.
bio: Ian McIntyre led Syracuse to the 2022 NCAA Championship — one of the most significant titles in the program's history and the first in almost four decades. His teams play technically organised, pressing-based soccer with strong defensive structure. The 2022 title and the 2025 ACC semi-final run demonstrate the program ceiling under McIntyre. He has built Syracuse into a consistent ACC contender with a strong recruiting pipeline from the New York metro region.

name: Jamie Davies
record: UNF: 2 consecutive ASUN titles, 2 NCAA Tournaments in 2 seasons from 3-win team
bio: Jamie Davies was appointed GCU head coach December 18, 2025 after one of the most remarkable coaching turnarounds in college soccer. At North Florida (ASUN), he inherited a 3-win team ranked 203rd nationally and built them to ranked #46 nationally with two consecutive conference titles and NCAA Tournament appearances. Prior to UNF, spent 6 seasons as UCF Associate HC — three AAC regular season titles, four NCAA Tournaments. Style: high-intensity pressing, technically demanding. GCU Sweet 16 run in 2025 under Kiefer gives him a strong foundation to build on.

name: Steve Clements
record: 408-57-23 at TJC; 554-90-26 total career
bio: Steve Clements is one of the most accomplished coaches in NJCAA history — 554-90-26 career record, 6 NJCAA DI National Championships at TJC (2009, 2010, 2012, 2014, 2016, 2017) and 1 at Meridian (2000). Second all-time in NJCAA wins, more wins than any active coach. Has built TJC into the #1 JUCO D1 transfer feeder in the country — 100+ professional contracts, 10 MLS draftees. Holds a USSF A Licence. No direct email listed; contact through assistant Shaun Stocker (ShaunStocker@tjc.edu / 903-510-2687).
strengths: 554-90-26 career record — most wins of any active NJCAA coach
#1 JUCO D1 transfer feeder nationally — all-time record for D1 placements
USSF A Licence — confirmed; 60 All-Americans, 7 National Players of the Year

name: Russell Payne
record: 5th season at Northwestern (since 2021); back-to-back winning seasons 2023-24, ranked as high as No. 9 nationally. Prior 11 seasons HC at Army West Point.
bio: Russell Payne has led Northwestern since 2021, guiding the Wildcats to back-to-back winning seasons in 2023-24 and a No. 9 national ranking. A former USMNT goalkeepers coach (2015-17) and U-23 assistant at the 2024 Paris Olympics, he spent 11 seasons as head coach at Army West Point and was an assistant on Maryland's national-championship staffs. Payne is a top-25 national recruiter (No. 5 class in 2022); Chicago proximity and Northwestern Medicine make it a strong pre-PT environment.

staff.name: JR DeRose
staff.bg: Assistant Coach / Director of Player Development.

name: Chaka Daley
record: 2025 Big Ten Tournament Final
bio: Chaka Daley joined Michigan after serving as US Youth National Team coach, bringing high-level tactical experience to Ann Arbor. His work has elevated Michigan to the 2025 Big Ten Tournament Final. Movement Science program partnership and Michigan Medicine pre-PT access make this a strong academic environment for Olivier.

name: Kylie Stannard
record: Ivy League competitor. Yale men's soccer in the most academically selective conference in college athletics.
bio: Head Coach Kylie Stannard leads Yale men's soccer in the Ivy League — zero athletic scholarships but one of the most academically elite environments in American sport. Yale's pre-health science infrastructure and DPT pathway credentials are outstanding. The program develops technically excellent players in a high-academic environment. The primary barrier for Olivier is financial: no athletic scholarships means the full ~5k/yr sticker price applies, offset only by need-based aid. Stannard is building Yale into a consistent Ivy contender alongside traditional powers Princeton and Penn.

name: Keith Wiggans
record: 27-31-18 overall, 10-19-8 CAA. 2024 best season: 10-6-2, ranked #23 nationally.
bio: Keith Wiggans enters his 6th season as HC in 2025. CofC alum (2000-04) who played 7 seasons for Charleston Battery (USL). 2026 staff from official roster: Jet Terry (Asst, hired March 2025 — former CofC player, was asst at SVSU where they won D2 conf title), Ernesto Aguinaga-Hernandez (Asst, joined July 2025 from Wisconsin-Parkside). Program email: msoccer@cofc.edu

name: Jay Vidovich
record: Former Wake Forest HC (2007 NCAA Champion). Pitt: 2024 ACC Regular Season Champions.
bio: Jay Vidovich has an elite coaching resume — he led Wake Forest to the 2007 NCAA Championship and spent over a decade building the Demon Deacons into a consistent national contender. Now at Pittsburgh, he quickly established the program as an ACC force: Pitt won the 2024 ACC Regular Season Championship. His possession-based, technically refined system has historically produced professional players and created winning cultures at multiple programs. Known for disciplined defensive organisation paired with technical quality in possession.

name: Zac Newton
record: 2025 NJCAA DI National Champions; 2024 national semifinal; first-ever No. 1 national ranking
bio: Zac Newton is one of the fastest-rising JUCO coaches in the country. In just his second season at Indian Hills (2025-26), he delivered the NJCAA DI National Championship — defeating Daytona State 1-0 in the final. His debut season (2024-25) produced the first-ever No. 1 national ranking (5 consecutive weeks) and first-ever national semifinal appearance. Previously won the 2021 NJCAA DII National Championship at Southeastern Community College. Named United Soccer Coaches Midwest Region Staff of the Year (2024). 11 players committed to 4-year universities in 2024-25.
strengths: 2025 NJCAA DI National Champion — fastest program rise in JUCO history
Proven transfer pipeline: 11 players to 4-year universities in one season
Previously won 2021 NJCAA DII National Championship at Southeastern CC
United Soccer Coaches Midwest Region Staff of the Year (2024)

name: Leonard Griffin
record: 2023 Pac-12 Coach of the Year. Led Cal's 2025 ACC Tournament semi-final run. Prior HC at Grand Canyon (2021 WAC title, NCAA berth) and San Francisco.
bio: Leonard Griffin, the 2023 Pac-12 Coach of the Year, has led Cal since 2022, guiding the Golden Bears through their transition from the Pac-12 into the ACC. A UCLA national champion as a player (2002) and former MLS pro (Chicago Fire, LA Galaxy), he has developed 35+ players into the professional ranks and previously was head coach at Grand Canyon (2021 WAC title, NCAA berth) and San Francisco. Strong Bay Area recruiting network; UCSF clinical access supports a pre-PT pathway.

name: Johnny Torres
record: 49-39-19 overall; NCAA Tournament 2021-22; 2022 College Cup (Final 8)
bio: Johnny Torres has been Creighton's head coach since November 2018, now in his seventh season. A Creighton alum and two-time National Player of the Year who played on the 1996 College Cup team, Torres has built a record of 49-39-19 including NCAA Tournament appearances in 2021 and 2022 and a return to the College Cup in 2022. Omaha's CHI Health and Nebraska Medicine create strong pre-PT/Chiro clinical access in the Jesuit academic environment.

name: Matt McArthur
record: SSC competitor. Consistent D2 Sunshine State Conference program in Fort Lauderdale.
bio: Head Coach Matt McArthur leads Nova Southeastern University in the highly competitive Sunshine State Conference — one of the strongest D2 conferences nationally alongside SSC rivals PBA, Barry, and Lynn. NSU is a research-focused private university in Fort Lauderdale with strong health sciences infrastructure including an accredited DPT program on campus. McArthur builds a technically solid program in a warm South Florida environment.

name: Joe Avallone
record: 2025 NJCAA DI National Championship finalists (lost 1-0 to Indian Hills)
bio: Joe Avallone was named Head Coach at Daytona State in 2023, bringing over 30 years of coaching experience. He co-founded and served as Director of Coaching at Florida Kraze/Krush Academy — one of Florida's premier youth development academies. In his second full season (2025), Avallone led the Falcons to the NJCAA DI National Championship final, losing 1-0 to Indian Hills CC. Focuses on player development and competitive NJCAA Region 8 play.
strengths: 2025 NJCAA DI National Championship finalists — elite program credibility
Florida Kraze/Krush Academy founder — deep Florida youth soccer network
30+ years coaching experience — proven long-term developer
Top-5 JUCO D1 transfer feeder — strong D1 placement track record

name: Chris Gbandi
record: UConn 2025 Big East Final; 6yrs HC at Northeastern; Hermann Trophy 2000; #1 MLS pick 2002
bio: Chris Gbandi was appointed UConn head coach in December 2021, returning to the program where he won the 2000 Hermann Trophy and helped the Huskies claim the NCAA title. A #1 overall MLS pick in 2002, he played nine years professionally before building his coaching career — assistant at UConn (2011-12), Holy Cross (2012-14), and Dartmouth (2014-16), then six years as head coach at Northeastern. He led UConn to the 2025 Big East Final in his fourth season. UConn Health Center remains one of the finest sports medicine and pre-PT environments in the Big East.

name: Oige Kennedy
record: 2024 Big West Coach of the Year. Back-to-back Big West regular-season titles (2024 program's first, 2025). Prior Stanford assistant during the Cardinal's national-championship era.
bio: Oige Kennedy, the 2024 Big West Coach of the Year, has led Cal Poly since 2022, directing the Mustangs to back-to-back Big West regular-season titles in 2024 (the program's first) and 2025 — the 2025 side matched a program record with 10 shutouts. A former Stanford assistant on Jeremy Gunn's staff during the Cardinal's national-championship era, he helped develop multiple MLS draft picks. San Luis Obispo's learn-by-doing kinesiology model pairs strong lab experience with soccer development.
strengths: 2024 Big West Coach of the Year

name: Stephen Roche
record: Tactical lead for 4 straight CAA titles (2021-24); HC since 2026
bio: Stephen Roche was promoted to head coach in January 2026 after 10 seasons on staff — the tactical and recruiting lead behind Hofstra's four straight CAA titles (2021-24) and a 114-47-31 run. A Dublin native and former League of Ireland pro, he has developed six MLS draftees (incl. Sean Nealis, Ryan Carmichael, Eliot Goldthorp) and multiple All-Americans. NYC via LIRR (30 min) is an extraordinary competitive advantage.

name: Marcus DiBernardo
record: 2021, 2022, 2023 NJCAA D1 National Champions — 3 consecutive national titles.
bio: Marcus DiBernardo leads the Monroe Mustangs (New Rochelle campus) — the premier NJCAA D1 men's soccer program in the US with 3 consecutive national championships (2021-2023). The 2025 roster includes two Australians: Edward Mackle (Perth, WA — midfielder) and Sebastian Sayers (Hillarys, WA — forward). DiBernardo has built a world-class recruiting network drawing players from 15+ countries. New Rochelle is 30 minutes from Manhattan by Metro-North rail. Note: a separate Bronx campus program (Monroe Express, Coach Jose Hernandez) also exists — this entry covers the Mustangs only.

name: Kyle Segebart
record: Building UCA into ASUN contender. International recruiting focus.
bio: Head Coach Kyle Segebart has assembled an internationally competitive roster at UCA, drawing from Europe (France, Czech Republic, Portugal, Russia, Italy, Spain, Denmark, Japan), North America, and Central America. The 2025 squad is graduate-student heavy — meaning Segebart faces a significant rebuild before 2027, with 6 of 9 midfielders departing. This creates the most open central midfielder opportunity of any D1 program in the guide. Segebart runs a reserve/JV programme at UCA — additional development minutes available beyond the varsity squad. His staff includes assistants Joe LaCasto and Will Montgomery plus Director of Soccer Operations Travis Crane. The UAMS clinical network partnership makes UCA a genuine pre-PT pathway — Segebart has likely developed talking points around the DPT pipeline for recruiting health sciences students.

staff.name: Joe LaCasto
staff.bg: Assistant coach — player recruitment and tactical development.

staff.name: Will Montgomery
staff.bg: Assistant coach contributing to player development and scouting.

staff.name: Travis Crane
staff.bg: Director of operations — logistics, recruiting coordination, programme administration.

staff.name: Johnathan Hull
staff.bg: Certified athletic trainer for men's soccer. UAMS clinical network integration.

name: Joey Worthen
record: 2025 AAC Tournament Champions in debut season. First NCAA Tournament for FAU since 2007.
bio: Joey Worthen achieved something remarkable in his first season as FAU head coach — leading the Owls to the 2025 AAC Tournament Championship and their first NCAA Tournament appearance since 2007. This breakout result signals genuine momentum for a program transitioning from Conference USA to the AAC. FAU's unique selling point for Olivier is not the soccer pedigree but the degree: the Pre-PT/OT concentration in Exercise Science & Health Promotion, with a formal articulation agreement giving students a guaranteed early interview for FIU's DPT program. This is the most direct physical therapy pathway of any school in the entire guide. Boca Raton's South Florida lifestyle — warm year-round, beach access, close to Miami and Fort Lauderdale — is the best climate match for Olivier's Sydney background.
strengths: Pre-PT degree with FIU DPT guaranteed pathway

name: Neil Jones
record: HC at Wisconsin since 2022 (8-win 2025 season). Prior 9 seasons at Loyola Chicago: 82-54-26, two Missouri Valley titles, NCAA Tournament appearances.
bio: Neil Jones has led Wisconsin since 2022, improving the Badgers to an eight-win 2025 season. An Auckland, New Zealand native and former New Zealand youth international, he spent nine seasons as head coach at Loyola Chicago (82-54-26, two Missouri Valley Conference titles), was part of UC Santa Barbara's 2006 NCAA Championship staff and served as Northwestern's associate head coach during its Big Ten title runs. USSF-licensed with USA national-team scouting experience; Madison offers strong minutes and UW Health clinical access.
strengths: Two MVC titles at Loyola Chicago

name: Jim McElderry
record: Multiple Big East titles, 2025 RPI #38
bio: Jim McElderry is long-tenured at Rutgers navigating the Big Ten. His 2025 season produced an RPI of #38 — above the program's Big Ten standing. NYC proximity and the NY Red Bulls relationship create unique professional pathways for Rutgers players.

name: Michael Erush
record: CCAA competitor. Cal State LA D2 program in East Los Angeles.
bio: Head Coach Michael Erush leads Cal State LA with a full staff of four assistants — Associate HC Dallas Jaussi, and assistants Erick Lemus, Eduardo Castellanos, and Christian Segovia. A four-person staff is exceptional at D2 CCAA level. Cal State LA is the most affordable option in Los Angeles at ~8k/yr, giving access to LAFC and LA Galaxy academies, the same clinical network as the city-based D1 programs, and the cultural diversity of East LA. Erush's contact email: merush@calstatela.edu.

staff.name: Dallas Jaussi
staff.bg: Associate HC — recruitment and player development.

staff.name: Eduardo Castellanos
staff.bg: Assistant coach — tactical and opposition analysis.

staff.name: Christian Segovia
staff.bg: Assistant coach — player welfare and recruitment.

name: Brian Plotkin
record: 2022 Patriot League Coach of the Year; won program's first Patriot League regular-season title in 26 years (2022). 6th season in 2026.
bio: Brian Plotkin, the 2022 Patriot League Coach of the Year, enters his sixth season leading Army West Point, having won the program's first Patriot League regular-season title in 26 years in 2022. A two-time national champion as a player at Indiana (2003, 2004), All-American and MLS Cup winner, he holds a USSF 'A' license and coached previously at Notre Dame and Dartmouth. ⚠ Service academy — a 5-year US Army service commitment post-graduation makes it incompatible with a DPT/Chiro or MLS pathway; listed for completeness.
strengths: 2022 Patriot League Coach of the Year

name: Jamie Clark
record: Multiple Pac-12 titles, beat UCLA 2025
bio: Jamie Clark is building Washington through the Pac-12 to Big Ten transition. His 2025 team beat UCLA (eventual Big Ten Tournament champions) at home. Seattle Sounders MLS relationship creates a genuine pro pathway. UW Medicine is elite for pre-PT/Chiro development.

name: Gavin Oldham
record: Head coach at Keiser University. NAIA Sun Conference. Health sciences integrated program.
bio: Head Coach Gavin Oldham leads the Keiser University men's soccer program in Fort Lauderdale. Keiser is a health sciences university — the clinical simulation labs and pre-PT academic environment are core selling points for a player like Olivier targeting a DPT career. The program sits in the NAIA Sun Conference with generous scholarship potential and warm year-round climate. Fort Lauderdale provides proximity to Inter Miami, MLS action, and the South Florida professional soccer ecosystem. Oldham is supported by Assistant Coach Joe Barber and Goalkeeping Coach Leo Camacho, with athletic trainer Vittoria Giordano providing sports medicine support.

staff.name: Joe Barber
staff.bg: Assistant coach contributing to player development and recruitment.

staff.name: Leo Camacho
staff.bg: Dedicated goalkeeping coach — specialist GK development within the Sun Conference program.

staff.name: Vittoria Giordano
staff.bg: Sports medicine support for men's soccer. Keiser health sciences facilities available to student-athletes.

name: Daniel Smee
record: Building D2 MEC program. International recruitment specialist.
bio: Head Coach Daniel Smee has built an internationally diverse squad at University of Charleston WV, drawing players from Argentina, Brazil, Spain, France, Greece, Poland, the UK, New Zealand, Colombia, Chile, Costa Rica, Kenya, China, and Australia. Jack Allan (Cape Schanck, VIC) is a current Australian senior on the roster — confirming an active pipeline from Australia. Smee's seven-person coaching staff (including a tactical advisor and dedicated video analyst) is professional in setup for a D2 program. For Olivier, the Australian precedent is the key talking point — Smee already understands how to recruit, scholarship, and develop Southern Hemisphere athletes in the MEC environment. Program is building toward MEC contention.

staff.name: Corbin Bowers
staff.bg: Assistant coach at UC Charleston. International recruitment background.

staff.name: Connor Hartwell
staff.bg: Assistant coach contributing to player development.

staff.name: Mohammad Rad
staff.bg: Volunteer tactical advisor — professional coaching input on game model.

staff.name: Mattia Vezzoni
staff.bg: Dedicated video analyst — uncommon at D2 level, shows program professionalism.

name: David Cameron
record: 2025 NJCAA DII National Champions; ranked #1 nationally for 7 consecutive weeks (2025); 3 consecutive Region I titles (2021-2023); 6 straight national tournament appearances
bio: David Cameron enters his 21st season as Head Coach at Phoenix College — one of the most successful active JUCO men's soccer coaches nationally. Winner of the 2025 NJCAA DII National Championship while ranked #1 nationally for 7 consecutive weeks, with 3 consecutive Region I titles (2021-2023) and 6 straight national tournament appearances. Named 2025 ACCAC Coach of the Year.
strengths: 21-year tenured head coach — deepest institutional experience of any JUCO coach in the guide
Confirmed dedicated Exercise Science degree on campus

name: Lee Avery
record: CCCAA / SCFA competitor. Premier JUCO to UCLA transfer pipeline in California.
bio: Head Coach Lee Avery has built Santa Monica College into the premier JUCO feeder to UCLA in California. The 2025-26 roster reflects a diverse international recruiting network — predominantly Latin American and Spanish-speaking players with international contributors including a French midfielder (Leo Blanchet), a Brazilian defender (Daniel Loff Fernandes), and Greek twin brothers George and Greg Georgantellis. As a JUCO program, the entire current roster turns over before Olivier arrives in August 2027. Avery's documented relationships with UCLA soccer transfer advisors give SMC the strongest transfer pipeline of any JUCO in California.
strengths: Santa Monica lifestyle match for Sydney-raised players

name: Adrian Dubois
record: 1st season 2026 — inheriting 2024 NCAA National Champions
bio: Adrian Dubois was appointed head coach in 2026 after Rob Dow departed following Vermont's extraordinary 2024 NCAA National Championship — the first title in any UVM sport outside skiing. Dubois inherits a program with major graduation losses from the championship squad and significant pressure to continue Dow's winning culture. Burlington VT is cold and remote — but the soccer tradition is now elite-level.

name: Michael McBride
record: 65-43-8 career record at Blinn; ranked #8 nationally (NJCAA DI, Oct 2025); 26 years of collegiate coaching experience
bio: Michael McBride enters his 6th season as Head Coach at Blinn College, holding a 65-43-8 record and 26 years of collegiate coaching experience. Assistant coach Jacob Beerman holds a Bachelor of Science in Exercise Science (2015).
strengths: On-campus RELLIS pathway to a BS in Kinesiology/Sport Management

name: Jeremy Bonomo
record: 3rd season at NU; 2x Horizon League COY & 75 wins in 9 seasons at Green Bay
bio: Jeremy Bonomo, a New England native, is in his third season leading Northeastern after being hired in February 2024. A two-time Horizon League Coach of the Year, he won 75 games over nine seasons as head coach at Green Bay — taking the Phoenix to the 2023 NCAA Tournament and a 2017 regular-season title — and previously head-coached Assumption. His defensively organized sides pair with Northeastern's co-op model, the best pre-PT clinical pathway in the CAA via paid rotations in Boston's hospital system (MGH, Brigham & Women's, Boston Children's).

name: Kevin Langan
school: University of North Carolina at Charlotte
record: 154-64-38 career at Charlotte; 11 NCAA Tournament appearances; Conference USA CoY 2016; 16 pros developed
bio: Kevin Langan is the all-time winningest coach at Charlotte with a 154-64-38 career record over 14+ seasons. He has led the 49ers to 11 NCAA Tournament appearances, earned Conference USA Coach of the Year in 2016, and developed 16 professional players including multiple All-Americans. A former professional player in England, Langan brings tactical sophistication and deep program knowledge. Charlotte ranked No.24 nationally in 2025 — a program with genuine upward trajectory in the AAC.

name: Marc Reeves
record: Multiple CAA titles (2022, 2025); building Elon into one of the most competitive mid-major soccer programs in the Southeast
bio: Marc Reeves has turned Elon into a genuine CAA power — back-to-back conference titles in 2022 and 2025 make the Phoenix one of the most decorated programs in the league over the last four years. Associate HC Jay O'Donnell adds staff depth. Elon's small liberal-arts environment in Burlington NC suits athletes who want a tight-knit campus culture with competitive D1 soccer. The Alamance Regional Medical Center proximity and Elon's pre-health advising network is the academic pitch for PT-track recruits.

name: Mike Brown
record: NJCAA Region XI competitor. Strong transfer pipeline to D1/D2.
bio: Head Coach Mike Brown leads a highly international JUCO program at Iowa Western CC. The 2025-26 roster features players from Japan (6), Canada (5), Spain (2), Brazil, Latvia, Germany, Chile, Sudan, and New Zealand — a genuinely global recruiting operation. Brown's staff includes Associate Head Coach Cesar Herrera, Assistant Coach Anthony Galeana, GK Coach Caleb Hinson, and Director of Performance Grant Johannes — a full professional setup rare at JUCO level. The key question for Olivier is simple: the entire current roster turns over before he arrives in August 2027 (JUCO is a 2-year programme), so Brown's 2026 recruiting class is the only competition that matters. Ask him directly: 'How many central midfielders are in your 2026 class?' Brown's international track record suggests he would be receptive to an Australian recruit.

staff.name: Cesar Herrera
staff.bg: Associate head coach — international recruitment and player development focus.

staff.name: Anthony Galeana
staff.bg: Assistant coach contributing to technical development.

staff.name: Caleb Hinson
staff.bg: Dedicated GK coach — uncommon at JUCO level.

staff.name: Grant Johannes
staff.bg: Dedicated performance director — strength, conditioning and sports science integration.

name: Tommy McMenemy
record: Competitive CAA program; player development focus; building Blue Hens into a consistent conference threat
bio: Tommy McMenemy leads the Delaware Blue Hens with an emphasis on player development and competitive CAA performance. Delaware's July 2025 move to Conference USA is a key programme note — Olivier should confirm current conference standing. Newark DE location offers proximity to the Philadelphia MLS ecosystem (Union) and the University of Delaware's Division of Physical Therapy for pre-DPT track athletes.

name: David Korn
record: 8yrs HC at D2 Maryville (98-38-26, 6 consecutive NCAA tournaments, 3 GLVC titles); D1 debut at Marquette 2024
bio: David Korn was named Marquette head coach in December 2023, arriving after eight years at D2 Maryville where he compiled a 98-38-26 record with six consecutive NCAA tournament appearances and three GLVC titles. Now in his second season at D1 level, Korn brings a strong development pedigree to the Froedtert & Medical College of Wisconsin partnership that provides pre-PT clinical access unique in the Big East. His program builds on the Jesuit culture that makes Milwaukee a strong environment for international recruits.

name: Dino Raso
record: 94-96-23 all-time. 8 CACC postseason appearances. 2 CACC Championship Final appearances.
bio: Dino Raso enters his 13th season at Georgian Court in 2025-26 — one of the longest-tenured coaches in CACC D2 history. A loyalist builder who has established GCU as a consistent CACC postseason contender. The program's explicit Pre-PT/OT/PA track in Exercise Science is the strongest academic match at D2 level in the NJ/NY region. Associate HC Robbie Fitzpatrick handles much of the day-to-day training management.

name: Yossi Raz
record: Multiple Big West Coach of the Year awards; conference titles; consistent NCAA berths; 2025 Big West Tournament Champions
bio: Yossi Raz has been at UC Irvine for 10+ seasons and is one of the most decorated coaches in Big West Conference history — multiple Big West Coach of the Year awards, conference championships, and consistent NCAA Tournament appearances. His 2025 squad won the Big West Tournament title (2-1 over UCSB in the final). Deep Orange County recruiting network and UCI Medical Center (nationally ranked academic medical centre) gives Raz strong pre-PT pathway talking points with international recruits.

name: Andreas Lindberg
record: LIU-Post (D2) 139-30-15, 4x NCAA East Region champ; Seton Hall HC since Dec 2017
bio: Andreas Lindberg has been Seton Hall's head coach since December 2017, now in his eighth season. Swedish-born and USSF 'A' Licence holder, he built D2 LIU-Post into a powerhouse over nine years (139-30-15, eight ECC titles, four NCAA East Region championships) before joining the Pirates. The New Jersey location provides excellent access to NYC Red Bulls and NYCFC (30-45 min) and Hackensack Meridian Health sports medicine facilities.

name: Kenny Dale
record: 4x ACCAC Champions, 3x NJCAA Region I Champions; 2021 undefeated season (17-0); ranked #2 nationally (NJCAA DI, Oct 2025)
bio: Kenny Dale leads a nationally ranked (#2, 2025) program backed by one of the most structured coaching staffs among guide JUCOs — Assistant Bello Alhassan, Assistant/Recruiting Coordinator Admir Balicevac, Assistant Coach/Europe Tim Dennison, and Volunteer Assistant Tom vonAhlefeld. The 2025-26 roster of 30 draws from France, Japan, Canada, Ghana, Senegal, Brazil, Switzerland, Netherlands, Italy, Colombia, and Ethiopia.
strengths: 5-person coaching staff incl. dedicated European recruiting coach
Genuinely relevant Exercise, Wellness and Nutrition degree
Warm, sunny Arizona desert climate at one of the guide's most affordable price points

name: David Cosgrove
record: 433 career wins (most in program history); 2021 and 2018 NJCAA national championships; ranked #6 nationally (NJCAA DII, Oct 2025)
bio: David Cosgrove has coached at Pima Community College since 1998, winning NJCAA national championships in 2018 and 2021 and holding the most wins (433) in program history.
strengths: 433 career wins — most in program history

name: John Higgins
record: 12yrs HC at D2 Univ of Indianapolis (150-56-29); Xavier HC since March 2022
bio: John Higgins was named Xavier's seventh men's soccer head coach in March 2022, joining from D2 University of Indianapolis where he compiled a 150-56-29 record over 12 seasons. Now in his fourth season at D1 Xavier, Higgins is building the program in the Big East with FC Cincinnati (MLS, 10 min from campus) as a key professional development pathway. Cincinnati's UC Health and Cincinnati Children's Hospital (top-4 USA) provide strong clinical access for pre-PT students.

name: Chris Norris
record: 166-169-60 (22 seasons); 2 CAA titles (2010, 2017), 4 NCAA appearances
bio: Chris Norris is one of the longest-tenured coaches in Division I — a 1995 W&M graduate and former Tribe defender, now in his 23rd season as head coach (36th on staff overall). He owns a 166-169-60 record with CAA Championships in 2010 and 2017, four NCAA Tournament berths and a 2010 Sweet Sixteen run, and was 2010 CAA Coach of the Year. One of the most selective public universities in the US; GPA 3.0+ required. Historic Williamsburg campus.

name: Craig Stewart
record: Mid-table Big East, NCAA Tournament appearances
bio: Craig Stewart manages Providence's place in the Big East with solid mid-table consistency. Providence is a small, high-academic Friar Dominican university in a top US healthcare corridor. Brown and Lifespan Health are walkable from campus, creating exceptional pre-PT/Chiro clinical access for student-athletes.

name: Kelly Findley
record: Building NC State as a mid-tier ACC program. 2025: 3rd seed ACC Tournament.
bio: Kelly Findley has been steadily developing NC State's standing in the ACC with energetic, pressing-oriented soccer. The 2025 ACC Tournament 3rd seed finish — the program's best in years — reflects the growing quality of the Wolfpack roster. Findley's teams are known for high work-rate defending and direct transitions. The Raleigh market is one of the fastest-growing youth soccer regions in the South, providing strong local recruitment.

name: Giuseppe DePalo
record: NJCAA Florida competitor. Transfer pipeline to FIU and Barry University.
bio: Head Coach Giuseppe DePalo leads a highly international JUCO program at Miami Dade College. The 2025-26 roster draws from Argentina, Italy, Brazil, France, and Spain — a South American and European recruiting network uncommon at JUCO level. DePalo’s son Matteo is on the 2025-26 roster as a freshman midfielder, reflecting a deep personal investment in the program. Like all JUCO programs, the entire current roster turns over before Olivier arrives in August 2027. DePalo’s South American network may be receptive to an Australian recruit. Transfer connections to FIU (AAC D1) and Barry (SSC D2 national title contender) are the primary pathway assets.

name: Marcos Vinicius Longo Ribeiro
record: 5x KJCCC Conference Champions, 5x NJCAA Region VI Champions, 5x Plains District Champions; #4-5 nationally ranked (2025)
bio: Marcos Vinicius Longo Ribeiro leads one of the most decorated JUCO soccer programs in the guide — 5x KJCCC, 5x Region VI, and 5x Plains District champions, currently ranked #4-5 nationally (NJCAA DI, 2025). Assistant coach Eusebio "Chevi" Plaza. The 2025-26 roster is heavily Japanese and Brazilian.
strengths: Most decorated conference/region/district title record among guide JUCOs (5x/5x/5x)

name: Tom McIntosh
record: 293-210-63 career; 12 conference titles; 12 NCAA appearances incl. quarterfinals; 10+ 10-win seasons
bio: Tom McIntosh is a Tulsa legend — 31st season, all-time winningest coach in program history (293-210-63), 12 conference championships, and 12 NCAA Tournament appearances including quarterfinal runs. A former player and assistant at Tulsa, he embodies the program's identity. Remarkable longevity at one school is a green flag for athlete development and stability. The pressing, physical style he has developed suits a box-to-box midfielder profile.

name: Dwayne Shaffer
record: Multiple Big West titles; consistent NCAA appearances; 2024 Big West Tournament Champions; one of the most respected veteran coaches in the conference
bio: Dwayne Shaffer is in his 29th season at UC Davis — an extraordinary tenure making him one of the longest-serving active head coaches in US college soccer. He has built Davis into a Big West power with multiple conference titles and NCAA Tournament appearances, crowned by the 2024 Big West Tournament Championship. His stability and deep program knowledge is a major asset. UC Davis Medical Center and the Sacramento biomedical ecosystem provide the pre-PT clinical access that makes this a legitimate DPT-pathway program.

name: Austin Solomon
record: Men's head coach since 2023 (described as "second-year head coach" by Mercyhurst in Nov 2024). Won the NEC regular-season title in the program's first D1 season (2024, 5-1-2); 8th of 10 in 2025.
bio: Austin Solomon has led Mercyhurst men's soccer since 2023, having joined the staff in 2013 as a graduate assistant, becoming assistant coach in 2015 and associate head coach in 2018. In the program's first NCAA D1 season after its move from D2 (PSAC) to the Northeast Conference in Fall 2024, Solomon's Lakers won the NEC regular-season title (5-1-2, 17 pts) and his staff was named NEC Coaching Staff of the Year, with Dylan Sumner taking NEC Player of the Year. The 2025 follow-up was hard: ranked 1st in the NEC preseason poll, they finished 8th of 10 (3-5-1). Staff includes Andrew Bennett (Associate HC), Logan McFadden (player development), and Ethan Kinyo (GK coach).
strengths: Won the NEC regular-season title in the program's first D1 season (2024)
2024 NEC Coaching Staff of the Year
Exercise Science degree with clinical internship

name: Ben MacRae
record: First-ever ICCAC regular season championship (2025); ranked #7 nationally (NJCAA DII, Oct 2025)
bio: Ben MacRae is Head Men's Soccer Coach at Iowa Lakes Community College, leading the program to its first-ever ICCAC regular season championship and a #7 national ranking (NJCAA DII, 2025). Staff: Bryan Marshall (assistant), Garrett Barnes (GA), Jesse Kuehler (JV HC), Nick Erdmann (goalkeepers).
strengths: Confirmed dedicated Exercise Science & Kinesiology Transfer Major
Large, well-resourced coaching staff (5 coaches including JV program)

name: Damon Rensing
record: Consistent Big Ten mid-table
bio: Damon Rensing is a long-tenured MSU coach holding the program in mid-table Big Ten. Best minutes opportunity in the Michigan region for international recruits who cannot crack elite Big Ten programs.

name: John Michael Hayden
record: Developing Louisville in the ACC. Focus on international recruiting.
bio: John Michael Hayden is actively building Louisville's competitive profile in the ACC with a strong focus on international recruitment — 3 MLS picks in 5 years shows growing program quality. Hayden's teams play energetic, transition-focused soccer with pressing structure. Louisville's position as the lowest-cost ACC program (COA ~$45k) combined with a genuine city campus and the UofL Health pre-PT environment makes this an increasingly attractive proposition for international players who fit the athletic profile.

name: Juan Espinal
record: Ranked #12 nationally (NJCAA DI, Oct 2025)
bio: Juan Espinal is Head Men's Soccer Coach at Dodge City Community College, leading a nationally-ranked (#12 DI, 2025) and heavily international roster spanning Japan, Jamaica, Italy, New Zealand, Honduras, South Africa, and Grenada. No detailed coaching tenure/record is published on the official athletics site.
strengths: Genuinely international roster and recruiting network
Cheapest tuition in the guide's newest JUCO batch

name: Jeff Cole
record: 2025 NJCAA DII Region 6/9 tournament champions; ranked #15 nationally (NJCAA DII, Oct 2025)
bio: Jeff Cole is Head Men's Soccer Coach at Johnson County Community College, leading the program to the 2025 NJCAA DII Region 6/9 tournament title (defeating previously-unbeaten Neosho County CC in the final) and a #15 national ranking.
strengths: Overland Park, KS — genuine Kansas City metro campus location

name: Sam Hall
record: Head Men's Soccer Coach (confirmed via official staff directory), succeeding Elliot Chadderton. Program ranked #4 nationally (NJCAA DII) in 2025.
bio: Sam Hall is Head Men's Soccer Coach at Neosho County Community College — confirmed via the official athletics staff directory (shall@neosho.edu), succeeding Elliot Chadderton. He inherits a program that ranked #4 nationally (NJCAA DII) in 2025 with a heavily international roster spanning England, Japan, Denmark, Germany and Colombia. Detailed coaching tenure and career record are not yet published on the official athletics site.
strengths: Athletic Training academic program on campus

name: Mark Plotkin
record: Lower Big East, Chicago location
bio: Mark Plotkin is developing DePaul's program in Chicago's Lincoln Park neighborhood — one of the best urban campus environments in US college soccer. Chicago Fire (MLS), Advocate Health, and Northwestern Medicine all nearby. DePaul's Vincentian mission and Chicago medical district create strong pre-PT pathway. Program is lower Big East but the city environment is unmatched.

name: David Castellanos
record: Penn State Abington Hall of Fame inductee; strong international recruiting pipeline; program culture builder
bio: David Castellanos brings a strong international recruiting background to Drexel Men's Soccer — recently inducted into the Penn State Abington Hall of Fame for his coaching career. His emphasis on program culture and international pipeline aligns well with Olivier's profile as a Belgian international recruit. Drexel co-op program (6-month paid professional placements in Philadelphia hospitals and research centres) is the strongest pre-PT academic differentiator in the CAA.

name: Bart Sasnett
record: 2021 Region 8 Champions; 2024-25 Region 8 + Southeast District Champions, NJCAA DI National Tournament semifinalist (Final Four); back-to-back national tournament appearances (2024-25, 2025-26)
bio: Bart Sasnett led EFSC to consecutive NJCAA DI National Tournament appearances, including a Final Four run in 2024-25 — the program's 4th all-time semifinal appearance. Staff includes Assistant Adrian Moreno, Goalkeeper Coach Calvin Dixon, and Assistants Jean Ganter and Taylor Pitts. The program plays home matches at the 1,000-seat Titan Soccer Complex, which hosted Orlando City B (USL) matches in 2016.
strengths: Back-to-back NJCAA DI National Tournament appearances (2024-25 Final Four, 2025-26)
1,000-seat dedicated Titan Soccer Complex — pro-USL hosting history
Warm, coastal Space Coast Florida location

name: Henrique Vieira
record: Ranked #5 nationally (NJCAA DII, Oct 2025) — interim role
bio: Henrique Vieira serves as Interim Head Coach for Southeastern Community College men's soccer, leading a #5-nationally-ranked (NJCAA DII, 2025) program. Assistant coaches: Anthony Schroder, Emily Murphy.
strengths: Confirmed dedicated Exercise Science Transfer Major on campus

name: Keith Ginsberg
record: 2025 NJCAA Region 15 Champions (defeated Ulster County CC 2-0)
bio: Keith Ginsberg is Head Men's & Women's Soccer Coach at Suffolk County Community College, leading the men's program to the 2025 NJCAA Region 15 Championship. Assistant coaches: Frankie Guzman, Melany Pagan-Rosario, Paige Palazzolo.
strengths: Confirmed dedicated Exercise Science A.S. degree on campus
Also leads the women's program — dual-role institutional trust

name: Jeff Perry
record: Ranked #20 nationally (NJCAA DII, Oct 2025)
bio: Jeff Perry is Head Men's Soccer Coach at Glendale Community College, leading a 2025 roster ranked #20 nationally (NJCAA DII). No detailed coaching tenure/record is published on the official athletics site.
strengths: Confirmed dedicated Exercise Science degree on campus
On-campus NAU BS Health Sciences completion pathway

name: Ian Sarachan
record: First season at Butler (2026) — hired Nov 2025
bio: Ian Sarachan enters his first season as Butler's 7th head coach after 5 seasons as a Creighton assistant (2022 NCAA College Cup), stints at Wisconsin and Santa Clara, and most recently as an assistant with MLS's Colorado Rapids. No Butler head-coaching record yet, but a strong player-development pedigree. The Indianapolis campus has excellent pre-PT pathways through Indiana University Health and the IU School of Medicine. Butler is a small selective liberal arts college with very high academic standards.

name: Jon Pascale
record: Multiple CCAA titles; led UCSD through D2-to-D1 transition; competitive Big West performances post-elevation
bio: Jon Pascale has been at UC San Diego for 18+ seasons, guiding the program through one of the most significant transitions in West Coast college soccer — from D2 CCAA dominance to full D1 Big West membership. Multiple CCAA championship wins and a strong D1 competitive record post-elevation demonstrate his program-building ability. UCSD Health (top-15 nationally for research, Level 1 trauma, Rady Children's Hospital proximity) is Pascale's strongest pitch to pre-DPT/Chiro recruits — the best clinical access in the Big West.

name: Richard Mulrooney
record: Multiple NCAA Tournament appearances (2022 at-large, 2023); rebuilt program from mid-major obscurity; first 10+ win seasons in modern era
bio: Richard Mulrooney is a former MLS player (San Jose Earthquakes, FC Dallas, Houston Dynamo) and USMNT international who has rebuilt Memphis soccer from the ground up since 2014. He delivered the program's first back-to-back NCAA Tournament appearances (2022 at-large bid, 2023) and multiple 10+ win seasons. His professional playing background in top-level football gives him a tactical eye that punches above Memphis's conference standing. Memphis native — deep local and regional recruiting roots.

name: Bryan Green
record: 20+ years D1 experience; Associate HC at Louisville (3 NCAA appearances, ACC title); HC at Belmont
bio: Bryan Green brings 20+ years of D1 experience to Temple in his 4th season. Before Temple, he served as Associate Head Coach at Louisville (3 NCAA appearances, ACC title) and was previously head coach at Belmont. A former All-American player at Elizabethtown College, Green is known for structured player development and strong tactical preparation. Rebuilding Temple's AAC program with Philadelphia Union (MLS) proximity as a key recruitment asset.

name: Head Coach
record: Rebuilding — 2nd North CAA 2025 (after 0-6-2 in 2024)
bio: Stony Brook offers the best value proposition in the CAA: SUNY pricing ($38k) + Stony Brook Medicine on campus (Level 1 trauma, nationally ranked). Strong 2025 bounce-back after winless 2024. Long Island location with NYC accessible. Best pre-PT clinical access in CAA for the cost.

name: Adam Potter
record: NJCAA Division II — consistently competitive with near-100% international roster (29 players, 15+ countries).
bio: Adam Potter coaches the Northeast Community College Hawks — one of the most internationally diverse JUCO programs in the Midwest. The 2025-26 roster includes players from Denmark, Germany, Netherlands, Italy, Spain, England, Austria, Brazil, Japan, Ecuador, Iceland, Chile — and one Australian: Isaac Jordaan from Bridgewater, WA (defender, Freshman). A true transfer platform program. Best contact for Olivier: athletics@northeast.edu.

name: Rafael Simmons
record: 44-16-8 career record; 2025 NJCAA DI National Tournament qualifier, #4 nationally ranked (Oct 2025)
bio: Rafael Simmons enters his 4th season as Head Coach at Barton, with a 44-16-8 record and a 2025 NJCAA DI National Tournament berth (#4 national ranking). Assistant coaches Scott Gray and Carlos Sanchez (both 1st season). The 2025-26 roster of 36 draws heavily from Brazil, Japan, Germany, France, and Spain.
strengths: Dedicated Exercise Science degree on campus
Large, internationally diverse roster and recruiting network
One of the most affordable full-profile JUCOs (~$16.2k/yr)

name: Camilo Valencia
record: Ranked #16 nationally (NJCAA DI, Oct 2025) — first national ranking in program history
bio: Camilo Valencia joined Mohave Community College in August 2022 as founding Director of Soccer Programs and Head Coach, building the program from scratch to a #16 national ranking by 2025. Holds a master's degree in Exercise Science and Health Promotion (California University of Pennsylvania, 2016).
strengths: Built the program from scratch to a national ranking in 3 seasons
Access to new 126,500 sqft Anderson Auto Group Fieldhouse (2019)

name: Dan Fisher
record: Best 2025 regular-season record in NJCAA Region 15 (10-2)
bio: Dan Fisher is Head Men's Soccer Coach at Nassau Community College, leading the program to the best regular-season record in NJCAA Region 15 for 2025 (10-2). Staff: Christian Turizo, Damiano Diliberti, John Paul Hughes, Jordan Russolillo.
strengths: Garden City, NY — genuine NYC-metro access

name: David Lilly
record: SoCon Regular Season title + Coach of the Year at ETSU (2023); back-to-back NAIA Tournaments at Milligan
bio: David Lilly joined UAB in 2024 after a career-defining season at East Tennessee State — SoCon regular season title and SoCon Coach of the Year in 2023. A Scottish former professional player, Lilly previously took Milligan University to back-to-back NAIA Tournament appearances. His development track record and European playing background make him an interesting appointment at UAB, where the on-campus UAB Hospital (national research hospital) is a genuine pre-PT/Chiro recruitment differentiator.

name: Martin Melchor
record: 11-5-1 overall (2025), 3rd Region 14 South Division
bio: Martin Melchor is Head Men's Soccer Coach at Angelina College, leading a 2025 roster to a solid 11-5-1 record (3rd in Region 14 South). No detailed coaching tenure/record is published on the official athletics site.
strengths: Plays in the guide's newest dedicated soccer stadium (Larry Phillips Family Sports Complex, 2022)

name: Nathan Mason
record: Appointed March 2025. First AMC tournament appearance in program history in 2024.
bio: Nathan Mason was named Head Men's Soccer Coach at Columbia College (Columbia, MO) in March 2025. He inherits a small NAIA program (~35 players) in a vibrant university town dominated by the University of Missouri. Columbia College offers full-scholarship NAIA potential with no equivalency cap — the main selling point alongside the Mizzou college-town lifestyle. The absence of an exercise science degree is the key limitation for Olivier's pre-PT pathway.

name: Justin Rodriguez
record: Ranked #14 nationally (NJCAA DI, Oct 2025) — under prior interim staff; hired March 2026
bio: Justin Rodriguez was announced as Head Men's Soccer Coach at Coastal Bend College in March 2026, succeeding interim coach Manuel Iwabuchi (appointed August 2025). Previously coached at Missouri Southern State University (2024) and as an assistant at University of the Ozarks (2021); collegiate player at Schreiner University (2017-2021).
strengths: New head coach bringing fresh Division-level coaching experience

name: Jamal Lis-Simmons
record: 9-1-2 regular season; 2025 Region 15 Championship runner-up
bio: Jamal Lis-Simmons is Head Men's Soccer Coach at Ulster County Community College, leading the program to a 2025 Region 15 Championship final appearance (9-1-2 regular season, lost 2-0 to Suffolk CC). Assistant coach: Sal DeRosa.
strengths: Strong local Hudson Valley recruiting network

name: John Plumbar
record: 6-8-1 overall (2025), 2nd season as Head Coach
bio: John Plumbar was hired as Head Men's Soccer Coach at LSU Eunice in July 2024, entering his 2nd season in 2025-26. Assistant coaches: Braeden Hoppough, Lane Woodall.
strengths: Bengal Village — well-resourced on-campus housing (216 beds)

name: Alfio Carrabotta
record: 9-1-2 conference record (2025), T-3rd/4th in Region 15
bio: Alfio Carrabotta is Head Men's Soccer Coach at Westchester Community College, leading a 2025 roster to a 9-1-2 conference record (tied for 3rd/4th in Region 15). Assistant coaches: Carlos Zabarbaru, Luis Lua.
strengths: Valhalla, NY — genuine Westchester County / NYC-metro access

name: Dustin Johnson
record: SCIAC competitor. D3 program in Orange County, CA. No athletic scholarships.
bio: Head Coach Dustin Johnson leads Chapman University men's soccer in the SCIAC — a strong D3 Southern California conference. Chapman's value for Olivier is academic: the KIN405 Pre-PT required course is the most explicit pre-PT unit in the guide. Orange County location, warm climate, and small private university culture make it appealing for lifestyle fit. The financial reality is the barrier: D3 means zero athletic scholarships — admission-based and need-based aid only. Best suited for a player who prioritises the PT pathway and Orange County lifestyle over soccer ambition.
strengths: KIN405 Pre-PT required course (unique in guide)

name: Robert McCourt
record: Building competitive CAA program at the NJ Shore; strong regional and international recruiting presence
bio: Robert McCourt is building a competitive CAA programme at Monmouth University on the New Jersey Shore — a unique environment combining suburban NJ recruiting access with a coastal campus setting (1 mile from the Atlantic). Strong recruiting presence across the northeast and internationally. Monmouth's new School of Health Studies (nursing, physical therapy pathway) is the academic hook for pre-health recruits. Associate HC Hugh MacDonald (hmacdona@monmouth.edu, 732-263-5837) is a useful secondary contact.

name: George Kuntz
record: 30+ year D1 veteran; built UC Irvine into Big West power before joining Fullerton; 12 MLS players developed; multiple Big West titles across programs
bio: George Kuntz is a 30+ year D1 veteran who previously built UC Irvine into a Big West power (4 NCAA appearances, multiple conference titles) before joining CSU Fullerton. In 11+ seasons at Fullerton, he has developed 12 MLS players and maintained competitive Big West standing. Orange County location (Huntington Beach 15 min, CHOC clinical access, Disneyland 3 min) and Kuntz's development track record make this a credible choice for maximum-minutes-seeking international recruits.

name: Mark Fetrow
record: Former MLS player (Vancouver 2011); Villanova assoc HC 2019-23; led Drexel 9-2-5 (2023); Villanova HC since Nov 2023
bio: Mark Fetrow was named Villanova head coach in November 2023, returning to a program where he spent four years as associate head coach (2019-23). A former MLS player drafted by Vancouver in 2011, Fetrow led Drexel to a 9-2-5 record and CAA tournament berth in his one season there before coming home to Villanova. Now in his second season, he is rebuilding a program that has finished last in the Big East East Division three consecutive years. Philadelphia's medical district (CHOP, Penn Medicine) remains one of the best pre-PT clinical environments in the guide.

name: Tim Cupello
record: Building competitive Big West program at UCR; Pac-12/Big West partnership announced 2025
bio: Tim Cupello leads UC Riverside men's soccer as the program navigates a significant moment — the Big West/Pac-12 partnership for men's soccer announced in 2025 raises the conference's competitive profile considerably. UCR is the most affordable UC campus option in the guide and sits in the Inland Empire with strong community college transfer pipeline. Riverside University Health System (county hospital network) provides credible pre-PT clinical access for health-track recruits.

name: John Hackworth
record: First season at Navy (2026) — service academy program
bio: ⚠ SERVICE ACADEMY: Naval Academy is fully funded D1 soccer with a 5-year US Navy service commitment post-graduation. Hackworth arrives after 4 years as director of coaching/technical director for MLS's St. Louis City SC, named 2025 MLS NEXT Pro Coach of the Year. Annapolis, MD is a beautiful historic city. Not compatible with DPT/Chiro career pathway or MLS goals. Listed for completeness — not recommended for Olivier.
